name: Periscopes
notes: Best with glassy or northerly wind and enough tide. Dry-season trades can blow it out, so mornings and light wind windows matter.
description: Periscopes is a right-hand reef wave east of the main Lakey Peak area. It peels along a reef platform into a channel, offering fast rippable walls and barrel sections without the same intensity as Lakey Peak or Lakey Pipe. It is a good progression wave for confident intermediates moving toward hollower reef breaks.
hazards: channel can close out on bigger swells
